---
Francesca Wade, [Gertrude Stein: An Afterlife](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=e830393380ef52917ed3dad348158f14f67c3dd36e3984d0623f914849f4ce49&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=58e0a10599b117128028dcffab63d63a&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811). One of the best-written biographies I have read in years. I would not say it is close to my core interests, but if you think you might like it you will.
Jacqueline Harpman, [I Who Never Known Men](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=cc5fe7ed5d6292cdbc9c232271a3b7732f4547e787399af17bf0af59c94ad73f&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=f6aaa09e67108cf7bbe1ffc1eddb25c6&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811). I enjoyed this novel: "Deep Underground, thirty-nine women live imprisoned in a cage. Watched over by guards, the women have no memory of how they got there, no notion of time, and only a vague recollection of their lives before."
J.P. Mallory, [The Indo-Europeans Rediscovered: How a Scientific Revolution is Rewriting Their Story](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=5a35bc49624e55d1bf13b6501af54e1b73938f02f3e5a7bcf8e0c485e0fc0c14&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=3fb3b8b3494fdaea8bca7a0880b79247&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811). The best book I know of on the origins and unities of Indo-European languages. I had not known Edward Sapir was born in Pomerania. And "...the Keres people who occupy seven pueblos (villages) in New Mexico speak a language totally unrelated to any of their neighbours and their origins have been frequently disputed."
Anthony Trollope, [Phineas Finn](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=0610b1777699110e2d5c83326e91a95255ffe9708485ebbe28b3c6997ef419e0&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=a895f54970fa90fa99e2b2a63eaa9b15&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811). One of the best Trollope novels it seems, even though it is not (yet?) clear what the plot actually consists of. [Currency decimalization is also one of the side plots](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=ba5be135159ae1a0aacd4fadb91f7840f44d35199f27a3fad1b4301fc1404131&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=a5b6c60bd4a5ac1fded69faae3492261&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9jaGF0Z3B0LmNvbS9zaGFyZS82ODUzZGRiMi04Mzk0LTgwMTAtODY5NC1mMDQyMDRmZThjNzE&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811), who can argue with that?
Manu S. Pillai, [Gods, Guns and Missionaries: The Making of the Modern Hindu Identity](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=aeb599c7c254d881972c1fc8b9db01b0125c8ecd16d32e83e0274579620233e9&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90770&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=2e002def00f3a98daad54f0382dde7bb&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=f8901d35483ee3431139db98ac78b811). It may be fruitless to argue about this topic on-line, but almost all Westerners under-read when the topic is Hinduism.
